Class CombinationQueueArray

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.CombinationQueueArray
public class CombinationQueueArray extends Object
A structure for managing work distribution and shared resources between generators and monkeys.

This class serves as the central coordination point for the solver. It has two primary responsibilities:

  1. Work Distribution: It holds an array of CombinationQueue instances, one for each monkey. This partitions the workload and reduces contention.
  2. Centralized Pooling: It manages a global workBatchPool for recycling WorkBatch objects. This is critical for minimizing garbage collection by allowing consumers to return processed batches to a central location for reuse by generators.

Architectural Role

As a singleton instance, this class provides the infrastructure for decoupling generators from monkeys. Generators acquire empty WorkBatch objects from the central pool, fill them with work, and offer them to one of the work queues. Monkeys poll their designated queue, process the entire batch, and return the now-empty WorkBatch to the central pool.

It also manages the application's lifecycle by tracking the state of the pool and signaling termination conditions via the solutionFound and generationComplete flags.

Memory and Performance

The entire structure is designed to minimize allocations and synchronization in the hot path. The workBatchPool is pre-allocated at startup to a size that guarantees a recycled batch is never discarded, eliminating the need for runtime allocations. Communication relies on lock-free queues from JCTools and volatile flags, ensuring high throughput.

Since:
2025.05 - Multiple CombinationQueues
See Also:
  • MpmcArrayQueue
Performance Characteristics:
O(1) for most operations, O(queues.length) for iterating through all queues.
Threading Model:
Thread-safe; uses lock-free structures and volatile flags for safe concurrent access and updates.
Memory Management:
Fixed memory footprint after initialization; no dynamic allocations in the hot path.

  • Field Details

    • instance

      private static volatile CombinationQueueArray instance
      The singleton instance of this class.

      This field ensures that only one instance of CombinationQueueArray exists throughout the application's lifecycle. It is initialized lazily via getInstance(int) to allow proper configuration with the number of consumers.

      Since:
      2025.10 - Singleton Enforcement
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; uses volatile for safe publication.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal footprint of 4 bytes as a reference.

    • queues

      private final CombinationQueue[] queues
      An array of work queues, with each queue dedicated to a specific monkey.

      This design partitions the workload, significantly reducing contention compared to a single shared queue. Each monkey primarily polls its own dedicated queue. The array structure also facilitates work-stealing, allowing an idle monkey to poll other queues for work.

      Since:
      2025.05 - Multiple CombinationQueues
      See Also:
      • getQueue(int)
      • getAllQueues()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access of an individual queue, O(queues.length) for iterating through all queues.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; immutable for read operations after construction.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal overhead of queues.length * 4 bytes as an array of references.

    • workBatchPool

      private final MpmcArrayQueue<WorkBatch> workBatchPool
      A central, thread-safe pool for recycling WorkBatch objects.

      This pool is the cornerstone of the application's memory management strategy. It allows WorkBatch instances to be reused, effectively eliminating them as a source of garbage collection pressure.

      The lifecycle is as follows:

      1. The pool is pre-filled with empty WorkBatch instances at startup.
      2. A generator polls a batch from this pool.
      3. The generator fills the batch with work items.
      4. The full batch is offered to a work queue.
      5. A monkey polls the batch, processes its contents, and clears it.
      6. The monkey returns the empty, cleared batch to this pool.

      The pool is sized to hold a number of batches equal to the total capacity of all work queues, guaranteeing that a recycled batch is never discarded.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Enqueuing WorkBatch Objects
      See Also:
      • getWorkBatchPool()
      • MpmcArrayQueue
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for both offer and poll operations, O(workBatchPool.capacity()) for size().
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe through JCTools wizardry
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint after initialization

    • startTime

      private final long startTime
      The start time of the program's operation, used for logging purposes. This timestamp is initialized during construction.
      Since:
      2025.10 - Elapsed Time Refactor
      See Also:
      • endTime
      • getStartTime()
      • StartYourMonkeys
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; immutable field.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal footprint of 8 bytes as a long.

    • endTime

      private long endTime
      The end time of the program's operation, used for logging purposes. This timestamp is set when a solution is found or generation is marked as complete, otherwise remains -1.

      This field piggybacks on the volatile writes to solutionFound and generationComplete for visibility, saving the overhead of an additional volatile write.

      Since:
      2025.10 - Elapsed Time Refactor
      See Also:
      • startTime
      • getEndTime()
      • StartYourMonkeys
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; piggybacks on volatile writes for safe publication.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal footprint of 8 bytes as a long.

    • winningMonkey

      private String winningMonkey
      The name of the monkey that found the solution. Written once when solutionFound is set.

      Performance Considerations

      This field piggybacks on the volatile write to solutionFound for visibility, saving the overhead of an additional volatile write.

      Since:
      2025.05 - Multiple CombinationQueues
      See Also:
      • solutionFound(String, short[])
      • getWinningMonkey()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; piggybacks on the volatile write to solutionFound for safe publication.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal footprint of 4 bytes as a reference.

    • winningCombination

      private short[] winningCombination
      The click combination that solves the puzzle. Written once when solutionFound is set.

      Performance Considerations

      This field piggybacks on the volatile write to solutionFound for visibility, saving the overhead of an additional volatile write.

      Since:
      2025.05 - Multiple CombinationQueues
      See Also:
      • solutionFound(String, short[])
      • getWinningCombination()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; piggybacks on the volatile write to solutionFound for safe publication.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal footprint of 4 bytes as a reference.

    • solutionFound

      private volatile boolean solutionFound
      A volatile flag indicating that a solution has been found.

      Performance Considerations

      A volatile boolean is used instead of an AtomicBoolean because it provides the necessary visibility guarantees for a single-writer/multi-reader scenario with less overhead. It is read frequently in the hot path.

      While generators now rely on the ForkJoinPool's cancellation mechanism, the monkeys still poll this flag to terminate work. A future implementation could potentially unify this under a single cancellation mechanism, but this flag remains a simple and effective solution.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Volatile Flag Implementation
      See Also:
      • getWinningCombination()
      • getWinningMonkey()
      • solutionFound(String, short[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for reads and writes.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; uses volatile for safe concurrent access.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 1 byte as a boolean.

    • generationComplete

      private volatile boolean generationComplete
      A volatile flag indicating that all producers have completed.

      This is set to true when all generators have finished. It signals to monkeys that no new work will be added to the queues. A monkey can safely terminate when this flag is true and all work queues are empty.

      Performance Considerations

      A volatile boolean is used instead of an AtomicBoolean because it provides the necessary visibility guarantees for a single-writer/multi-reader scenario with less overhead. It is read frequently by monkeys to determine when to stop work.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Volatile Flag Implementation
      See Also:
      • TestClickCombination.allQueuesEmpty()
      • TestClickCombination.run()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for reads and writes.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; uses volatile for safe concurrent access.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 1 byte as a boolean.

  • Constructor Details

    • CombinationQueueArray

      private CombinationQueueArray(int numConsumers)
      Constructs the shared queue array and its associated resources. As a private constructor, it enforces the singleton pattern via getInstance(int).

      This constructor initializes the entire communication and resource-sharing infrastructure. It creates the array of work queues and pre-allocates the central WorkBatch pool. The pool is sized to match the total capacity of all work queues combined, which is a critical invariant to ensure the recycling mechanism never fails. Finally, it starts the timer for program execution by initializing the startTime field.

      Parameters:
      numConsumers - The number of monkeys. This determines the number of work queues to create.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if numConsumers is not positive.
      IllegalStateException - if the pre-allocation of the pool fails, indicating a configuration issue.
      Since:
      2025.05 - Multiple CombinationQueues
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(numConsumers) queue initialization + O(numConsumers * CombinationQueue.QUEUE_SIZE) pool pre-allocation = O(numConsumers) time complexity.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to instance isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates the array of queues, counters, and flags, and pre-allocates the pool.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static CombinationQueueArray getInstance(int numConsumers)
      Returns the singleton instance of CombinationQueueArray, initializing it with the specified number of consumers if it has not been created yet.

      This method enforces the singleton pattern, ensuring that only one instance exists. If the instance has already been initialized, subsequent calls will return the existing instance regardless of the numConsumers parameter. This allows the instance to be configured once at application startup.

      Parameters:
      numConsumers - The number of monkeys. Used only for initialization if the instance does not yet exist.
      Returns:
      The singleton instance of CombinationQueueArray.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if numConsumers is not positive (only on first call).
      IllegalStateException - if the pre-allocation of the pool fails (only on first call).
      Since:
      2025.10 - Singleton Enforcement
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for subsequent calls; O(numConsumers) for initialization.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; uses double-checked locking for lazy initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate unless initializing the singleton.

    • getWorkBatchPool

      public MpmcArrayQueue<WorkBatch> getWorkBatchPool()
      Returns the central pool for recycled WorkBatch objects.

      Both generators and monkeys use this method to access the pool. Generators poll from it to get empty batches, and monkeys offer to it to return processed batches.

      Returns:
      The thread-safe, multi-producer/multi-consumer queue used for pooling WorkBatch instances.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Enqueuing WorkBatch Objects
      See Also:
      • workBatchPool
      • CombinationQueueArray(int)
      • MpmcArrayQueue
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; returns a reference to an immutable field.

    • getQueue

      public CombinationQueue getQueue(int idx)
      Returns the work queue at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      idx - The index of the queue to retrieve.
      Returns:
      The CombinationQueue at the specified index in the queues array.
      Since:
      2025.05 - Multiple CombinationQueues
      See Also:
      • CombinationQueueArray(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; returns a reference to an immutable field.

    • getAllQueues

      public CombinationQueue[] getAllQueues()
      Returns the entire array of work queues.

      This is primarily used to enable work-stealing, where a monkey can iterate through other queues to find work if its own queue is empty.

      Returns:
      The array of all CombinationQueues.
      Since:
      2025.05 - Monkey Work-Stealing Introduction
      See Also:
      • CombinationQueueArray(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; returns a reference to an immutable field.

    • generationComplete

      public void generationComplete()
      Marks the completion of the generators. This method is called by the system when the last generator finishes its work, marking the completion flag as true.
      Since:
      2025.05 - Multiple CombinationQueues
      See Also:
      • StartYourMonkeys.main(String[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) compare and set via synchronized block.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; uses synchronized block with double-checked locking to ensure thread safety.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • solutionFound

      public void solutionFound(String monkeyName, short[] winningCombination)
      Signals that a solution has been found.

      This method sets the solutionFound flag to true and records the winning combination, the name of the thread that found it, and the current time. It is designed to be called by any monkey. A check ensures that only the first-found solution is recorded.

      Performance Considerations

      This method is called only once. A simple check on the solutionFound flag prevents a race condition where multiple monkeys find a solution simultaneously. A volatile boolean is used for the flag over an AtomicBoolean to reduce overhead, as the single-write nature of the event does not require atomic compare-and-set operations. The other fields could piggyback on this write for visibility.

      Parameters:
      monkeyName - The name of the monkey that found the solution.
      winningCombination - The combination that solves the puzzle.
      Since:
      2025.05 - Multiple CombinationQueues
      See Also:
      • endTime
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) check and set.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; uses synchronized block with double-checked locking to ensure thread safety.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • getWinningMonkey

      public String getWinningMonkey()
      Returns the name of the monkey that solved the puzzle, or throws if no solution has been found yet.
      Returns:
      The winning monkey's name.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no solution has been found yet.
      Since:
      2025.05.23 - Multiple CombinationQueues
      See Also:
      • getWinningCombination()
      • solutionFound(String, short[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; piggybacks on the volatile write to solutionFound for visibility.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates an exception if called before a solution is found.

    • getWinningCombination

      public short[] getWinningCombination()
      Returns a clone of the combination that solves the puzzle, or throws if no solution has been found yet.
      Returns:
      A clone of the winning combination array.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no solution has been found yet.
      Since:
      2025.05.23 - Multiple CombinationQueues
      See Also:
      • solutionFound
      • getWinningMonkey()
      • solutionFound(String, short[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; piggybacks on the volatile write to solutionFound for visibility.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates an exception if called before a solution is found, and allocates a clone of the array when returning.

    • getStartTime

      public long getStartTime()
      Returns the start time of the program's operation, used for logging purposes. This timestamp is initialized during construction.

      The start time in milliseconds since the epoch.
      Since:
      2025.10 - Elapsed Time Refactor
      See Also:

      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; returns a immutable field.

    • getEndTime

      public long getEndTime()
      Returns the end time of the program's operation, used for logging purposes. This timestamp is set when a solution is found or generation is marked as complete. Otherwise, it remains -1.
      Returns:
      The end time in milliseconds since the epoch, or -1 if not finished.
      Since:
      2025.10 - Elapsed Time Refactor
      See Also:
      • startTime
      • getStartTime()
      • StartYourMonkeys.main(String[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; performs a read of a volatile field.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

      public boolean isGenerationComplete()
      An accessor for the generationComplete flag, giving external classes read access to the flag without direct exposure.

      Threading Model:

      Memory Management:

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
